description:
## Goal

Add fast, deterministic tests at the Rust RPC orchestration seam and apply the small transition/UI cleanups identified in review.

## Required implementation

- Make `switch_workspace` orchestration testable with controllable command execution and transition capture. Prove Pending publishes before commands run, stale completion emits no failure/success transition, and snapshot changes only with a durable commit. Reuse or carefully consolidate `FakeStore`/`FakeRunner` test support only where it reduces real duplication; do not force a broad test framework rewrite.
- Centralize the TypeScript retryable switch-failure classification used by `App` and `WorkspaceSelector`.
- Improve the `fail_switch_request`/`WorkspaceService::fail_request` collaboration if a clearer return shape can eliminate duplicated current-generation reasoning without changing stale-result semantics.
- Add regression coverage for delayed same-generation Pending after both retryable and non-retryable failures; preserve the intended inline/banner presentation.
- Normalize newly touched Rust test names to one descriptive style.
- Add a focused test for `pickerDefaultPath` when no Current Workspace exists but the MRU catalog has available/unavailable entries.

## Constraints

Keep `WorkspaceService` as the durable state-machine seam. Do not implement the renderer reducer in `bsm-kia.6` here. Do not add test-only production hooks or fragile timing-dependent desktop tests.

## Validation

Run Rust tests/check/fmt, `pnpm test`, TypeScript checking, scoped Ultracite, and the affected desktop suite if transport behavior changes.
title: Harden workspace transition orchestration tests
